info.title: Enphase Monitoring API
info.description: Enphase Monitoring API provides access to system details, device data, production/consumption metrics, alarms, and events for solar energy systems.

get.summary: Fetch systems

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page to be returned. Default=1, Min=1. For example, if page is set to 2, 2nd page is returned

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of records shown per page. Default=10, Min=1, Max=100.  For example, if max is set to 5, 5 records are shown per page

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Returns list of systems sorted by <sort_by> field. To get the ASC order sorted list, user sort_by = id. To get the DESC order sorted list, use sort_by = -id. By default, the list is sorted by ascending order of system ID.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page to be returned. Default=1, Min=1, e.g=2

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of records shown per page. Default=10, Min=1, Max=1000, e.g=5

parameters.name: live_stream
parameters.description: Indicates whether the live_stream field should be included in the response (true to include, false to omit).

get.summary: Retrieves a System by id

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system. If an empty value is passed in the ID, this endpoint behaves as Fetch systems endpoint.

get.summary: Retrieves a system summary

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: summary_date
parameters.description: Requested date for the system's summary data. Defaults to the current local date of the system

get.summary: Retrieves devices for a given system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

get.summary: Retrieve system for a given envoy serial number

parameters.name: serial_num
parameters.description: Envoy Serial Number

get.summary: To retrieve the list of events for a site

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Requested start time of the events data in Epoch time format.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Requested end time of the events data in Epoch time format. Defaults to minimum (start time + 1 day, current time)

get.summary: To retrieve the list of alarms for a site

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Requested start time of the alarms data in Epoch time format.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Requested end time of the alarms data in Epoch time format. Defaults to minimum (start time + 1 day, current time)

parameters.name: cleared
parameters.description: Filters alarms based on their status. Set to true to return cleared alarms; defaults to false to return active alarms.

get.summary: To retrieve the list of event_type_id along with event_description and recommended_action

parameters.name: event_type_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the event type.

get.summary: To retrieve the list of all open events of a site

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system.

get.summary: production_meter_readings

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: end_at
parameters.description: End of reporting period in Unix epoch time. If no end is specified, defaults to the time of the request. If the end is later than the last reported interval the response data ends with the last reported interval

get.summary: consumption_meter_readings

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: end_at
parameters.description: End of reporting period in Unix epoch time. If no end is specified, defaults to the time of the request. If the end is later than the last reported interval the response data ends with the last reported interval

get.summary: storage_meter_readings

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: end_at
parameters.description: End of reporting period in Unix epoch time. If no end is specified, defaults to the time of the request. If the end is later than the last reported interval the response data ends with the last reported interval

get.summary: rgm_stats

500.description: Under some conditions, data for a given period may be temporarily unavailable. When this condition is detected, the response code is 500 and the response body is:

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The numeric id of the system

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start of period to report on in Unix epoch time. If no start is specified, the assumed start is midnight today, in the timezone of the system. If the start is earlier than the system’s operational_date, the response data begins at midnight of the first reported interval date.

parameters.name: end_at
parameters.description: End of reporting period in Unix epoch time. If no end is specified, default to the time of the request or (start time + 1 week), whichever is earlier. If the end is later than the last reported interval the response data ends with the last reported interval.

get.summary: consumption_lifetime

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. Defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday if start_date is earlier than the system’s current date; it defaults to today if start_date is the current date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

get.summary: energy_lifetime

200.description: When the query parameters include “production=all”, returns meter and microinverter-measured time series:

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date of the time series data. Defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday if start_date is earlier than the system’s current date; it defaults to today if start_date is the current date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: production
parameters.description: When “all”, returns the merged time series plus the time series as reported by the microinverters and the meter on the system. Other values are ignored.

get.summary: battery_lifetime

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. Defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday if start_date is earlier than the system’s current date; it defaults to today if start_date is the current date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

get.summary: import_lifetime

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. Defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday if start_date is earlier than the system’s current date; it defaults to today if start_date is the current date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

get.summary: export_lifetime

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. Defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday if start_date is earlier than the system’s current date; it defaults to today if start_date is the current date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD.

get.summary: inverters_summary_by_envoy_or_site

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: Site id. The response will contain only those microinverters reporting to one of the active envoys of the given site.

parameters.name: envoy_serial_number
parameters.description: Envoy Serial Number. Only microinverters reporting to the given envoy will be present in the response.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page to be returned. Default=1, Min=1. For example, if page is set to 2, 2nd page is returned

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of records shown per page. Default=10, Min=1, Max=100.  For example, if max is set to 5, 5 records are shown per page

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for all production micros for a system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for all production meters for a system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String date format YYYY-MM-DD. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for all batteries for a system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: System id

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String date format YYYY-MM-DD. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for all consumption meters for a system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Requested start time for telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. If no start_at is specified, defaults to midnight today, in the timezone of the system. If the start_at specified is earlier than the system’s first reported date, then the system first reported date is considered as start_at. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for single micro/pcu.

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: System id

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the individual solar Microinverter

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry for single Encharge

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: System id

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the encharge

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Retrieves daily time-series telemetry data of the 3rd party EVSE charger

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The start_date defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for fetching the daily time-series data. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today.

get.summary: Retrieves daily time-series telemetry data of the HP

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The start_date defaults to the system’s operational date. If the start_date is earlier than the system’s operational_date, then the response data begins with the system operational_date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for fetching the daily time-series data. Pass as String date format YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today.

get.summary: Retrieves telemetry data of the EVSE charger at regular intervals.

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_num
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

parameters.name: interval_duration
parameters.description: Size of the interval. Can be 5mins, or 15mins. Site must be configured to 5 min telemetry for this to be supported

get.summary: Retrieves energy consumed by HP in regular intervals.

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String date format YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start Date in YYYY-MM-DD format

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

parameters.name: interval_duration
parameters.description: Size of the interval. Can be 5mins, or 15mins. Site must be configured to 5 min telemetry for this to be supported

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric id of the system

parameters.description: The unique numeric id of the system

parameters.description: The unique numeric id of the system

parameters.name: start_at

Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

parameters.name: start_at

Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYY-MM-DD format. By default start_at will appear in response. parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day', '15mins', '5mins'. Default is 'day'

get.summary: Site Level Live Status

401.description:
User will get 401 in the below cases. Response content type is application/json
NOT_AUTHENTICATED - API key is missing
UNAUTHORIZED - Access token is invalid
UNAUTHORIZED - Access token has expired
NOT_AUTHENTICATED - API Key is invalid
UNAUTHORIZED - Application is not authorized to access the requested resource

403.description: User is not authorized. Response content type is application/json

429.description: Too Many Requests. Response content type is application/json

461.description: Duration is less than 30 seconds. Response content type is application/json

462.description: Duration is greater then 900 seconds. Response content type is application/json

463.description: Duration is not integer. Response content type is application/json

466.description: Envoy must be active and envoy version must be at least 6.0.0. Response content type is application/json

468.description: System ID does not exist. Response content type is application/json

472.description: For Ensemble sites, live stream will be supported in the following cases - If site has an active battery or active system controller, then site must have  active production meter and active consumption meter; Otherwise, site must have active production meter. Response content type is application/json

473.description: IQ gateway is not reporting. Response content type is application/json

550.description: Service unreachable. Response content type is application/json

551.description: Service unreachable. Response content type is application/json

552.description: Unable to connect. Response content type is application/json

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Duration of the stream in seconds. Default=30, Min=30, Max=300, e.g=30

get.summary: Returns a system's last reported PV Power, Consumption Power, and Battery Power in Watts. Return’s operational mode for Battery, HP, and EVSE

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

get.summary: Fetch active chargers for a system

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

get.summary: Fetch events

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_num
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum limit: 100

get.summary: Charger session history

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum limit: 100

get.summary: Get Schedule

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

get.summary: Daily energy api

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. If the start_date is not provided, then the response data begins with the previous date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYYMMDD.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date of the time series data. Defaults to yesterday. Pass as String date format YYYYMMDD.

get.summary: Interval energy api

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: The granularity of the telemetry data. Possible values are 'week', 'day'. Default is 'day'

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Requested start date of the time series data. If the start_date and start_at is not provided, then the response data begins with the previous date as start_date. Pass as String date format YYYYMMDD.

parameters.name: start_at
parameters.description: Start time for fetching the telemetry data in Epoch time format. Alternatively one can use start_date in place of start_at for passing the start date as String in YYYYMMDD format. By default start_at will appear in response. If start_date parameter is passed in the url, then start_date field will appear in response.

post.summary: Start charging. Illustrative only - access via VPP api for partners

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger

post.summary: Stop charging. Illustrative only - access via VPP api for partners

parameters.name: system_id
parameters.description: The unique numeric ID of the system

parameters.name: serial_no
parameters.description: Serial Number of the EV Charger
